title: pueraria edulis
intro: Pueraria edulis, also known as the bitter pea or ashitaba, is a type of perennial legume native to East Asia, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. The health benefits of Pueraria edulis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, added to salads, or used in soups, with the young shoots and leaves being the most commonly used parts for herbal preparations.
